Project 01 · Current

Marine Power Optimization & Virtual Twin

MarineSitu · DOE / ORISE / WPTO Marine Energy Fellow · 2025—Present
Fig. 1.1Optimizer output: planned 7-day battery trajectory (left). ERA5-validated solar availability across 5 candidate deployment sites (right).

Problem

Plan and validate multi-day duty-cycle schedules for solar-powered, off-grid underwater monitoring platforms running cameras, sonar, UV-C antifouling, and acoustic sensors on battery alone.

Approach

Built a Python virtual twin coupling solar irradiance (ERA5 reanalysis), battery state-of-charge, and per-sensor power draw across multi-day deployments. Wrote three solvers (a greedy heuristic, a mixed-integer linear program, and a linear program) that produce optimized sampling schedules under battery, duty-cycle, and safety-floor constraints. Validated against ERA5 solar data across five candidate deployment sites from Hawaii to Anchorage.

Result

Tool produces deployable sampling schedules and identifies which deployment scenarios are feasible at each site and season, directly informing field deployment planning.

Skills & Stack
Python SciPy CVXPY MILP LP ERA5 reanalysis Battery modeling NVIDIA Jetson Edge computing
2024
Project 02

Multi-Component Alloy Development & Thermal Qualification

Zap Energy, Advanced Materials Group · 2024
Fig. 2.1Alloy development workflow with illustrative ternary phase diagram and DSC trace. Generic representation — no proprietary content.

Scope

Developed and qualified multi-component metal alloy systems and porous refractory-metal structures for extreme-environment thermal management hardware supporting fusion-energy component qualification.

Main Project · Thermophysical Property Evaluation for Liquid Metal Blanket Materials
  • Prepared research alloys in an inert atmosphere glovebox.
  • Investigated the eutectic composition of a novel family of alloys via thermal analysis with a Netzsch STA/TGA-DSC.
  • Evaluated alloy microstructure using SEM/EDX and optical microscopy.
Main Project · Porous Tungsten Structure Characterization
  • Conducted COMSOL simulations to evaluate structure porosity and permeability.
  • Measured structure density by Archimedes’ method.
Skills & Stack
Thermodynamic modeling Ternary eutectic alloys STA (TGA/DSC) SEM/EDX Glovebox ops Vacuum ops COMSOL porous media M&P workflows
2023
Project 03

Heat Transfer & Evaporation Studies

UCSD Thermal Energy Materials & Physics (TEMP) Lab · 2023
Fig. 3.1Test setup: schematic (A) and assembled rig (B).
Fig. 3.2Heat flux vs. T — CHF up to 600 W/cm² (GB-140). Figures from H. Zhang, M.S. Thesis, UCSD (2023).

Work performed

  • Performed evaporation experiments on AAO, glass fiber, and hydrogels — analyzing capillary pressure, permeability, and thin-film spreading to optimize heat transfer efficiency in high-performance cooling applications.
  • Troubleshot and resolved experimental issues including water transport inconsistencies, CHF deviations, and multimeter inaccuracies, ensuring precise data collection and system stability.
  • Applied advanced fluid mechanics principles such as the Young-Laplace equation and Darcy’s Law, demonstrating that optimized materials could sustain heat loads up to 600 W while mitigating scaling and evaporation inefficiencies.
Skills & Stack
Capillary flow AAO & hydrogel wicks Young-Laplace Darcy’s Law CHF characterization Python DAQ Root-cause troubleshooting
2021—22
Project 04

Falling-Film Evaporation & Pool Boiling Research

UIUC Energy & Multiphase Flow Lab · 2021—2022
Fig. 4.1Raw high-speed frame: falling-film over horizontal tube.
Fig. 4.2OpenCV pipeline output — thresholded for droplet & ligament segmentation.
Saltwater Falling-Film Evaporation & Anti-Fouling Surface Research · 2022
  • Conducted falling-film evaporation experiments on stainless steel and 3D-printed polymer tubes, evaluating the impact of surface coatings, wettability gradients, and groove patterns on scaling behavior and heat transfer efficiency.
  • Performed image processing and wave-velocity analysis in Python (OpenCV, Pandas) to quantify fouling-layer growth, liquid retention, and flow distribution.
  • Assisted in CFD model validation, manuscript editing, and data analysis, integrating heat- and mass-transfer principles to assess crystallization fouling mechanisms in thermal desalination.
Pool Boiling & Bubble Nucleation Research · 2021
  • Studied bubble-nucleation dynamics by testing stainless steel samples with varying micron-scale groove densities to analyze heat transfer performance.
  • Conducted pool-boiling experiments using an automated lab test system with DAQ, heater control, and high-speed imaging for data collection.
  • Analyzed nucleation-site distribution and thermal performance to assess material surface effects on boiling.
Publications Co-authored ASME J. Heat & Mass Transfer (2024) & Desalination (2023).
Skills & Stack
Pool boiling Falling-film evaporation Anti-fouling surfaces OpenCV Python DAQ Ansys Fluent High-speed imaging
—
Project 05 · Personal

Transient 2D Heat Conduction Solver

Personal project · Python / NumPy / Matplotlib
Fig. 5.1FTCS solver output: embedded heat source, fixed top/bottom/right, convective left BC.

Problem

Build a numerical model of an electronic chip dissipating heat into a 2D substrate with mixed boundary conditions — foundational for spacecraft electronics thermal control.

Approach

Implemented an explicit finite-difference (FTCS) scheme for the 2D transient heat equation on a 20×20 grid. Embedded a constant-temperature heat source; applied Dirichlet BCs on three sides and a convective (Robin) BC on the fourth. Built in a Fourier-number stability check and vectorized the inner loop with NumPy.

Result

Recovers the expected thermal field: heat-source spike, edge gradients, and convective cooling on the open boundary.

Skills & Stack
Python NumPy Matplotlib FDM / FTCS Transient conduction Stability analysis
